question_text: Two identical electromagnets use different batteries: one uses a 6V battery and the other uses a 12V battery. Which electromagnet will have the stronger magnetic field, and what is the scientific reason?

options.label: A
options.text: The 6V electromagnet will be stronger because lower voltages create more focused magnetic fields

options.label: B
options.text: The 12V electromagnet will be stronger because higher voltage causes greater current flow, which creates a stronger magnetic field

options.label: C
options.text: Both electromagnets will have the same strength because the coil design is identical

options.label: D
options.text: The 12V electromagnet will be stronger only if it also has an iron core

correct_answer: The 12V electromagnet will be stronger because higher voltage causes greater current flow, which creates a stronger magnetic field
explanation: When voltage increases in a circuit, the current also increases (assuming resistance stays the same). Since the strength of an electromagnet's magnetic field depends on the current flowing through its coil, higher current means a stronger magnetic field. The 12V battery produces more current than the 6V battery, so the 12V electromagnet creates a stronger magnetic field.
